name: Sudan

colors.symbolism: the struggle for independence and the blood of martyrs
peace, light, love, and the White Flag League nationalist movement of 1924
Sudan itself ('Sudan' is Arabic for 'black') and the historical Mahdist revolutionary flag
Islam, agriculture, and prosperity (the hoist triangle)

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red, white, and black — with a green isoceles triangle extending from the hoist edge.
history: Adopted on 20 May 1970 following the May 1969 revolution that brought Gaafar Nimeiry to power. The flag was selected from a public competition won by artist Abdel Rahman Ahmed Al-Jali. It replaced the blue-yellow-green tricolour of the post-independence period (1956–1970), aligning Sudan with the Arab Liberation tradition (red-white-black) used by Egypt, Syria, Iraq and Yemen, with the green triangle distinguishing it from those flags.

features.symbolism: the Pan-Arab colours of the Arab Liberation Flag, with a green hoist triangle added
